arxiv.abstract: Despite significant advances, AI systems struggle with the frame problem: determining what information is contextually relevant from an exponentially large possibility space. arxiv.title: Recent Advances in Transformer and Large Language Models for UAV Applications
arxiv.abstract: The rapid advancement of Transformer-based models has reshaped the landscape of uncrewed aerial vehicle (UAV) systems by enhancing perception, decision-making, and autonomy. arxiv.title: Rethinking Autonomy: Preventing Failures in AI-Driven Software Engineering
arxiv.abstract: The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into software engineering has revolutionized code generation, enabling unprecedented productivity through promptware and autonomous AI agents. arxiv.title: Contextual Attention-Based Multimodal Fusion of LLM and CNN for Sentiment Analysis
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces a novel approach for multimodal sentiment analysis on social media, particularly in the context of natural disasters, where understanding public sentiment is crucial for effective crisis management. arxiv.title: VideoAVE: A Multi-Attribute Video-to-Text Attribute Value Extraction Dataset and Benchmark Models
arxiv.abstract: Attribute Value Extraction (AVE) is important for structuring product information in e-commerce. However, existing AVE datasets are primarily limited to text-to-text or image-to-text settings, lacking support for product videos, diverse attribute coverage, and public availability. arxiv.title: A Multi-Task Evaluation of LLMs' Processing of Academic Text Input
arxiv.abstract: How much large language models (LLMs) can aid scientific discovery, notably in assisting academic peer review, is in heated debate. arxiv.title: The Rise of Generative AI for Metal-Organic Framework Design and Synthesis
arxiv.abstract: Advances in generative artificial intelligence are transforming how metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) are designed and discovered. This Perspective introduces the shift from laborious enumeration of MOF candidates to generative approaches that can autonomously propose and synthesize in the laboratory

arxiv.title: Labels or Input? Rethinking Augmentation in Multimodal Hate Detection
arxiv.abstract: Online hate remains a significant societal challenge, especially as multimodal content enables subtle, culturally grounded, and implicit forms of harm. arxiv.title: Uncalibrated Reasoning: GRPO Induces Overconfidence for Stochastic Outcomes
arxiv.abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) has proven remarkably effective at improving the accuracy of language models in verifiable and deterministic domains like mathematics. arxiv.title: When Does Language Transfer Help? Sequential Fine-Tuning for Cross-Lingual Euphemism Detection
arxiv.abstract: Euphemisms are culturally variable and often ambiguous, posing challenges for language models, especially in low-resource settings.
